title: Closure devices and container systems
abstract: One container system includes a transparent display, a projector for providing image data on the display, and a floor defining part of a watertight cavity. The display is non-perpendicularly angled from horizontal and has proximal and distal ends. And a closure device is provided for use with a watertight container. The closure device is configured to selectively seal an opening in the watertight container and includes coupling structure (a plug, a flange, and/or threading) for selectively coupling the closure device to the watertight container to seal the opening. The closure device further includes non-transitory computer memory, at least one sensor, and a processor in data communication with the memory and the at least one sensor.
claims:
Claims 1. A container system, comprising: a transparent display non-perpendicularly angled from horizontal, the display having proximal and distal ends; a projector for providing image data on the display; and a floor defining part of a watertight cavity. 2. The container system of claim 1, wherein: the display includes a plurality of transparent panels each having a proximal end, a distal end larger than the proximal end, and opposed sides; and each panel side is adjacent one of the sides of another panel, whereby forming a ring with each of the panel proximal ends at the display proximal end and with each of the panel distal ends at the display distal end. 3. The container system of claim 2, wherein the plurality of transparent panels is four transparent planar panels, each panel being generally symmetrical about a center line. 4. The container system of claim 2, further comprising: a top end forming part of the watertight cavity; a closure device configured to selectively seal an opening in the top end; and a biasing member coupled to at least one of the transparent panels for biasing the ring to an unfolded configuration; wherein the ring is foldable for insertion through the opening in the top end. 5. The container system of claim 2, wherein: the display distal end is closer to the floor than is the display proximal end; and the projector includes a plurality of image origination areas located radially outside the display proximal end. 6. The container system of claim 2, further comprising a reflective surface at the floor, and wherein: the display distal end is closer to the floor than is the display proximal end; and the projector includes a plurality of image origination areas located radially inside the display proximal end. 7. The container system of claim 2, further comprising: fluid in the watertight cavity; and at least one external wall extending upwardly from the floor to form an exterior perimeter; wherein the plurality of transparent panels form at least part of the watertight cavity; and wherein an area between the at least one external wall and the plurality of transparent panels is devoid of the fluid. 8. The container system of claim 2, wherein the plurality of transparent panels extend upwardly from the floor to define an exterior perimeter, the plurality of transparent panels forming at least part of the watertight cavity. 9. The container system of claim 8, wherein the exterior perimeter and the floor define at least part of a container selected from the group consisting of: a drinking glass, a bottle, a jug, a fish tank, and a display housing. 10. The container system of claim 8, further comprising: a top end forming part of the watertight cavity; and a closure device configured to selectively seal an opening in the top end; wherein the display distal end is closer to the floor than is the display proximal end; and wherein the closure device contains the projector. 11. The container system of claim 10, wherein the closure device further comprises: non-transitory computer memory; at least one sensor; and a processor in data communication with the computer memory, the at least one sensor, and the projector. 12. The container system of claim 2, further comprising at least one external wall extending upwardly from the floor to form an exterior perimeter defining part of the watertight cavity, the display being inside the exterior perimeter. 13. The container system of claim 12, wherein the exterior perimeter and the floor define at least part of a container selected from the group consisting of: a drinking glass, a bottle, a jug, a fish tank, and a display housing. 14. The container system of claim 12, further comprising a top end forming part of the watertight cavity and a closure device configured to selectively seal an opening in the top end, and wherein: the display distal end is closer to the floor than is the display proximal end; and the closure device contains the projector. 15. The container system of claim 12, wherein: the display proximal end is closer to the floor than is the display distal end; and the projector is positioned to provide the image data to the display proximal end before providing the image data to the display distal end. 16. The container system of claim 12, wherein: the display proximal end is closer to the floor than is the display distal end; the projector is positioned to provide the image data generally upwardly; and the floor is selectively positionable atop the projector, the image data being provided on the display only when the floor is positioned atop the projector. 17. The container system of claim 12, wherein: an outer portion of the watertight cavity extends outwardly from the plurality of transparent panels to the at least one external wall; an inner portion of the watertight cavity extends inwardly from the plurality of transparent panels; and at least one passage allows fluid to freely flow between the outer and inner portions of the watertight cavity. 18. The container system of claim 17, wherein the display distal end is closer to the floor than is the display proximal end. 19. The container system of claim 18, further comprising: a top end forming part of the watertight cavity; and a closure device configured to selectively seal an opening in the top end; wherein the closure device contains the projector. 20. The container system of claim 19, wherein the closure device further comprises a pour spout. 21. The container system of claim 19, wherein the closure device further comprises: non-transitory computer memory; at least one sensor; and a processor in data communication with the computer memory, the at least one sensor, and the projector. 22. The container system of claim 21, wherein the at least one sensor includes: at least one sensor for providing data about at least one quality characteristic of a fluid housed in the watertight cavity; at least one sensor for providing data about the quantity of the fluid; and 

 at least one sensor for providing data about whether the closure device is sealing the opening in the top end. 23. The container system of claim 1, further comprising: a top end forming part of the watertight cavity; and a closure device configured to selectively seal an opening in the top end; wherein the closure device comprises: non-transitory computer memory; at least one sensor; a processor in data communication with the memory and the at least one sensor; and coupling structure for selectively coupling the closure device to the top end to seal the opening, the coupling structure including at least one item selected from the group consisting of a plug, a flange, and threading; and wherein the at least one sensor comprises at least one item selected from the group consisting of: (a) at least one sensor for providing data about at least one quality characteristic of a fluid housed in the watertight cavity; (b) at least one sensor for providing data about the quantity of the fluid; and (c) at least one sensor for providing data about whether the closure device is sealing the opening in the top end. 24. The container system of claim 1, further comprising fluid in the watertight cavity. 25. A closure device for use with a watertight container, the closure device being configured to selectively seal an opening in the watertight container, the closure device comprising: coupling structure for selectively coupling the closure device to the watertight container to seal the opening, the coupling structure including at least one of a plug, a flange, and threading; non-transitory computer memory; at least one sensor; and a processor in data communication with the memory and the at least one sensor. 26. The closure device of claim 25, wherein the at least one sensor includes: at least one sensor for providing data about at least one quality characteristic of a fluid housed in the watertight container; at least one sensor for providing data about the quantity of the fluid; and at least one sensor for providing data about whether the closure device is sealing the opening. 27. The closure device of claim 26, further comprising: networking hardware in data communication with the processor; and an alarm output in data communication with the processor. 28. The closure device of claim 27, further comprising a projector in data communication with the processor. 29. The closure device of claim 28, further comprising a pour spout. 30. The closure device of claim 25, further comprising a projector in data communication with the processor.
